deviations.description: The change from 'patient_age 65+' to 'patient_age >65' in the precondition does not affect compliance, as the process model already checks if the patient is under 65 before discharge. The process only applies the discharge approval logic to patients over 65.

The new requirement introduces a precondition for patients with more than 5 medications, but the process model does not check or branch based on medication count before discharge. Patients with more than 5 medications can be discharged without any approval, violating the new requirement.

The new requirement obligates a doctor to approve discharge, but the process model only includes a nurse approval step before discharge. Discharge can occur without doctor approval, violating the new requirement.
The process model requires nurse approval for discharge of patients over 65, but the new requirement only requires doctor approval. Continuing to require nurse approval is stricter than necessary.
The process model enforces a 24-hour time constraint for approval before discharge, but the new requirement removes this temporal constraint. Enforcing this time limit is stricter than required.
The change in temporal validity period does not affect compliance, as it only shifts the rule's active dates and does not alter process behavior.
